name: Píñar and Iznalloz Almond Fields Loop
name_original: Campos de Almendros: Píñar y Iznalloz — circular desde Moreda
description: Cycle 34.5 miles through Granada's Montes Orientales, past almond fields and historic villages, with 1882 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Morelábor are set within the Comarca de los Montes in Granada, characterized by its distinctive landscapes. The region features rolling hills and ancient paths, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Alternating ridges and valleys define the area, with elevations reaching over 1,300 meters in features like La Serrezuela. These physical characteristics provide diverse road cycling experiences with notable elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Morelábor The most popular road cycling route is Píñar and Iznalloz Almond Fields Loop, a 34.5 miles (55.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through agricultural landscapes, offering a scenic ride. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mirador de la Magdalena – Cave Quarter, Guadix loop from Diezma, a moderate 43.5 miles (70.0 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, passing through varied terrain with historical points of interest. Local road cyclists also love the Roman Dam of Barcinas – View of Chulilla loop from Píñar, a 56.2 miles (90.5 km) trail leading through historical sites and natural viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Morelábor is defined by rolling hills, alternating ridges and valleys, and ancient paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Morelábor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 470 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Morelábor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Morelábor?
A: There are 11 road cycling routes around Morelábor, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 7 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Morelábor?
A: Road cycling in Morelábor is characterized by rolling hills, alternating ridges and valleys, and ancient paths. The region, situated in the Comarca de los Montes, features elevations reaching over 1,300 meters, providing diverse terrain with notable elevation changes suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Morelábor?
A: Yes, Morelábor offers several challenging routes with significant climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quéntar Reservoir – Hoya de la Mora loop from Diezma&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 166 km route with over 3,400 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14664357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Barcinas – View of Chulilla loop from Píñar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 90.5 km with over 1,000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders in Morelábor?
A: While there are no routes officially graded as 'easy', Morelábor does offer moderate routes that can be suitable for less experienced riders looking for a scenic ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986923596/pinar-and-iznalloz-almond-fields-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Píñar and Iznalloz Almond Fields Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 55.5 km route with around 570 meters of elevation gain, navigating through agricultural landscapes.

Q: What scenic points or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Morelábor?
A: The routes around Morelábor offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. You can cycle through the distinctive landscapes of the Geoparque de Granada, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6685775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road Through Geoparque de Granada&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like the Mudejar-style Parish Churches in Moreda and Laborcillas, and archaeological sites with well-preserved tombs.

Q: Are there historical sites to explore while cycling in Morelábor?
A: Yes, Morelábor has a rich medieval history. You can encounter Mudejar-style parish churches, such as the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Anunciación in Moreda, built in 1554. The area is also rich in archaeological sites, including tombs found in Cerro de los Castellanos and Cortijo de Fonseca, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Morelábor?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Morelábor, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to ancient paths, and the opportunities for invigorating rides with expansive views of the surrounding Andalusian countryside. The combination of physical activity and cultural discovery is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Morelábor?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Morelábor are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986923596/pinar-and-iznalloz-almond-fields-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Píñar and Iznalloz Almond Fields Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5289612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Magdalena – Cave Quarter, Guadix loop from Diezma&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular journeys.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Morelábor?
A: Given its location in northern Andalusia, Morelábor generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler mornings.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Morelábor?
A: As a rural area, Morelábor and its surrounding villages typically offer various parking options, especially in town centers or near popular starting points for routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for specific parking regulations.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: The traditional villages within and around Morelábor, such as Moreda and Laborcillas, often have local cafes or small shops where you can stop for refreshments. Many routes pass through or near these villages, providing opportunities to refuel and experience local culture.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Morelábor?
A: While there are no routes specifically designated as 'easy' for very young children, families with some cycling experience can enjoy the moderate routes. The varied terrain and scenic agricultural landscapes, such as those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31604097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Magdalena – Cave Quarter, Guadix loop from Pedro Martínez&lt;/a&gt;, can provide an engaging experience for active families.
tips: Píñar is a town surrounded by a large number of almond fields around one of the villages with a special charm. Basically because there is a set of natural caves in the town where its entrance is suitable, below the Castillo de Píñar.

Municipality with a large number of layers of history, where it was populated in remote times by an Iberian people, the Acatucitans, But the current toponym, Iznalloz, is of Arabic origin and means castle of the almond trees.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate for road cycling. It covers approximately 55.5 kilometers (34.5 miles) with an elevation gain and loss of about 570 meters (1,870 feet). While it features hills, it's not excessively mountainous, making it suitable for road bikes and cyclists with a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: When is the best time to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during late winter or early spring, particularly if you wish to experience the picturesque almond blossoms. The region is known for its almond groves, and seeing them in bloom is a significant seasonal highlight. Otherwise, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in the Montes Orientales region.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is named for the beautiful Almond Fields of Píñar, which are a major draw, especially when in bloom. You'll also pass through the historic town of Iznalloz, known for its Roman origins, a Roman bridge over the Cubillas river, and the remains of an Arab castle. The overall scenery includes olive groves and whitewashed villages.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts 'desde Moreda' (from Moreda), it's recommended to find parking within Moreda, Granada. As it's a local village, street parking or designated public parking areas should be available, though it's always good to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 36 minutes to complete by racebike. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed, fitness level, and any stops you make along the way to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the road due to safety concerns with traffic. If you plan to visit the towns or natural areas along the route, dogs may be permitted on leash, but always check local regulations for specific areas or establishments.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It traverses public roads and areas within the Montes Orientales region of Granada, Spain.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Moreda (the starting point) and the towns of Píñar and Iznalloz offer local amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and potentially small accommodations in these villages, providing opportunities for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths. You'll find it crosses sections of the Camino de Faucena, Cañada Real de la Atalaya de Cogollos, Cañada Real de Iznalloz a Guadix o de Venta Tejada, Camino Llano de la Valentina, Camino de los Morrones, and Camino de Prado Redondo.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and beautiful landscape characteristic of the Montes Orientales region. This includes extensive olive groves, charming whitewashed villages, and, depending on the season, picturesque almond fields in bloom. The area also features rich geological diversity and river systems, offering a blend of agricultural and natural beauty.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point in Moreda?
faqs.answer: Moreda, being a village in the Granada province, is typically accessible by local bus services from larger towns or Granada city. It's advisable to check current bus schedules and routes for the most up-to-date public transport options to reach Moreda.
